codeReview.summary: The follow-up fixes the stage-coverage half of the prior failure: `ti-data-pipeline-phase2` and `sa-premium-api` now have dedicated execution-plan entries, and the strengthened tests cover those omissions. But the proof-gate observability blocker is only partially addressed. Two previously non-observable proof gates remain in the dataset, so the rerun still falls short of VAL-ROADMAP-016 and cannot pass.

issues.description: The `pe-terminal-open` audit gate is still not consistently externally observable. Its criterion allows success when a security firm merely "provides BETTER" the report (`A named third-party security firm publishes or provides BETTER a completed audit report...`), which means the gate can still be satisfied through a private artifact instead of an investor-verifiable public proof surface. This was one of the original blocking observability gaps and the fix commit did not rewrite it.

The `re-enterprise-data` proof gate remains internally framed: `At least one institutional client completes a paid pilot with a signed data-licensing agreement and documented API usage over ≥ 30 days`. A signed agreement and documented usage can remain private to BETTER and the client, so this still is not an externally observable / investor-verifiable success criterion under VAL-ROADMAP-016.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Mission shared-state guidance still does not define which roadmap nodes count as "primary roadmap stages" for execution-plan coverage, so workers continue inventing local heuristics in code and tests.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `/Users/test/vision/.factory/library/content-model.md:71-75` requires execution-plan fields for each "major roadmap step", while `VAL-ROADMAP-016` requires coverage for each "primary roadmap stage". The fix encodes its own rule in `src/content/__tests__/execution-plans.test.ts:65-67` by asserting that every visible non-live/non-speculative roadmap node is a primary stage, but that convention is still not documented in mission shared state.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker marked `skillFeedback.followedProcedure=true`, but the transcript skeleton does not show the required `.factory/library/*.md` reads before editing this execution-plan feature.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `/Users/test/vision/.factory/skills/content-strategy-worker/SKILL.md:20-22` requires reading mission files and the relevant `.factory/library/*.md` files before changing anything. For worker session `5d66e932-4b1b-4acd-ba24-6ed0b53ef78a`, the transcript's recorded `Read` targets include mission files, `services.yaml`, and source/test files, but no `.factory/library/*.md` file before the first `Edit`; the handoff `/Users/test/.factory/missions/33dae310-26cf-4368-9488-64b235b6db83/handoffs/2026-03-23T03-46-04-484Z__investor-roadmap-execution-plan-coverage-and-proof-gate-observability-fix__5d66e932-4b1b-4acd-ba24-6ed0b53ef78a.json` still reports `"followedProcedure": true`.

summary: Fail. The rerun adds dedicated execution-plan treatment for the two omitted primary stages, but it does not finish the proof-gate observability repair: the audit-completion and enterprise-data proof gates still permit private/internal evidence rather than investor-verifiable public proof surfaces.
